title: HTML lex DFA extraction bug: state 76 doesn't stop at '<'
description:
## Root Cause

The `parseIfTransitions` function in `internal/generate/lex_extract.go` (lines 252-346) cannot handle **compound AND negation** conditions from the C parser.

### C Parser State 76 (HTML text content)
```c
if (lookahead != '<' && lookahead != 0) {
    ADVANCE(76);  // Keep consuming text
}
```

This means 'consume all chars except < and EOF as text'.

### What the Extraction Does Wrong
- Lines 283/291 explicitly exclude conditions with `||` but don't handle `&&`
- The compound `lookahead != '<' && lookahead != 0` falls through to the fallback (line 345)
- Fallback returns a generic transition with Char=0, IsNegated=false
- `detectDefault()` turns this into a catch-all `if !eof` check
- Generated code: `if !eof { state = 76; ... }` — consumes `<` as text

### Generated Code (HTML language.go state 76)
```go
case 76:
    lexer.MarkEnd()
    lexer.AcceptToken(16)
    // whitespace transitions...
    if !eof {  // <-- BUG: no guard against '<'
        state = 76
        lexer.Advance(false)
    }
    return true
```

### Fix Required
Update `parseIfTransitions` in lex_extract.go to detect and handle compound AND negations (`!=` with `&&`). Convert `lookahead != '<' && lookahead != 0` to a negated transition that excludes '<' AND EOF.

### Impact
- HTML parsing completely broken (text content consumes closing tags)
- May affect other grammars with compound AND negation lex conditions
- CSS appears unaffected (its conditions use simple patterns)

### Files to Change
- `internal/generate/lex_extract.go`: `parseIfTransitions` function
- After fix, regenerate HTML grammar: `internal/testgrammars/html/language.go`
- Enable skipped HTML tests in `scripting_integration_test.go`
